shorts.headline: U.S. and Iran Agree to 2-Week Ceasefire
shorts.narration: Breaking news—the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire, just hours before Trump’s deadline to launch massive strikes. This sudden diplomatic move could prevent a major escalation in the Middle East. Stay tuned for updates on this developing story.

shorts.headline: Stock Market Reacts to U.S.-Iran Tensions
shorts.narration: Oil prices are surging, but the Dow is dipping as the U.S. raises the heat on Iran. Investors are on edge, waiting to see how this ceasefire plays out. Will markets stabilize, or is more volatility ahead? Follow for live updates.

shorts.headline: Republican Clay Fuller Wins Special Election
shorts.narration: Clay Fuller has won the special election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene, but his narrow victory is giving Democrats reason to celebrate. This unexpected outcome could signal shifting political winds. What does this mean for the GOP’s future? Stay tuned.

shorts.headline: Telehealth Abortion Ruling Preserves Access
shorts.narration: A federal judge has ruled that telehealth abortion will remain available for now, blocking a Louisiana case seeking to ban mifepristone use via telemedicine. The FDA must review the safety of the abortion pill. This decision is a win for reproductive rights advocates.

shorts.headline: Aubrey Plaza Expecting First Child
shorts.narration: Parks and Rec star Aubrey Plaza is expecting her first child with Christopher Abbott, her costar from the 2020 film Black Bear. The beloved actress is stepping into a new chapter of her life. Congratulations are pouring in from fans and fellow celebrities alike!

shorts.headline: Wife of U.S. Soldier Released from Detention
shorts.narration: Annie Ramos, the wife of a U.S. Army staff sergeant, has been released from federal immigration detention after nearly a week. Her arrest inside a Louisiana military base sparked outrage. This case highlights the ongoing challenges faced by immigrant families in the U.S.

shorts.headline: Hawaii Investigates Rise in Strep Cases
shorts.narration: The Hawaii Department of Health is investigating a spike in strep infections in West Hawaii over the past few months. Officials are urging residents to take precautions as they work to identify the cause. Could this be a sign of a larger public health issue?

shorts.headline: South Korea Stocks Surge as Oil Prices Plunge
shorts.narration: Asia-Pacific markets are opening higher after Trump agreed to suspend planned attacks on Iranian infrastructure for two weeks. South Korea stocks are leading the gains as oil prices plummet. This ceasefire could bring much-needed stability to global markets.

shorts.headline: Retro Gadgets Inspired by the '80s
shorts.narration: Love the '80s? Amazon has a range of retro-inspired gadgets, from typewriter-style keyboards to CD players styled as record players. These nostalgic devices are perfect for anyone looking to add a touch of vintage flair to their tech collection.

shorts.headline: 'Oldest Octopus' Fossil Turns Out to Be Something Else
shorts.narration: Scientists have discovered that what was thought to be the oldest octopus fossil is actually something entirely different. Advanced scans revealed the truth behind this ancient mystery. Stay tuned for more details on this fascinating paleontological find.
